name: La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop
name_original: Puerto de El Gamoniteiro, El Cordar, Cuchu Puercu y La Cobertoria – desde La Pola
description: This difficult 53.6-mile road cycling loop in Asturias features steep climbs, mixed terrain, and 360-degree views from Gamoniteiro summit.
region_intro: Road cycling around Lena is characterized by mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. The region features numerous challenging climbs and descents, often traversing mountain passes and valleys. This landscape provides demanding routes for experienced road cyclists seeking high-altitude adventures. Best road cycling routes around Lena The most popular road cycling route is Angliru and Cordal Loop, a 34.9 miles (56.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and demanding ascents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cuitu Negru and Collado de Pajares – round trip from Campomanes, a difficult 32.5 miles (52.3 km) path. It is known for its challenging mountain passes and scenic vistas. Local road cyclists also love the La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop, a 53.6 miles (86.2 km) trail leading through mountain passes and high-altitude climbs, often completed in about 6 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Lena is defined by challenging mountain passes, high-altitude climbs, and deep valleys. The network offers options primarily for experienced road cyclists. The routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Lena?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Lena, offering a variety of challenging experiences for cyclists. The region is particularly known for its mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Lena?
A: Road cycling in Lena is characterized by mountainous terrain with numerous challenging climbs and descents. Routes often traverse mountain passes and deep valleys, providing demanding experiences primarily for experienced road cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Lena?
A: While Lena is known for its challenging routes, there are a few options suitable for less experienced riders. The guide lists 5 routes categorized as easy, though the overall region is geared towards more advanced cyclists due to its mountainous nature.

Q: What are some of the most scenic road cycling routes in Lena?
A: Many routes in Lena offer scenic vistas due to the mountainous landscape. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067421141/cuitu-negru-and-collado-de-pajares-round-trip-from-campomanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuitu Negru and Collado de Pajares – round trip from Campomanes&lt;/a&gt; is known for its challenging mountain passes and scenic views. The region's deep valleys and high-altitude climbs inherently provide stunning backdrops.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Lena?
A: Along the road cycling routes, you can encounter several notable natural features. These include impressive mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana Pass&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/102603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xiblu Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/859306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de San Salvador&lt;/a&gt; summit.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Lena?
A: The road cycling routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, high-altitude climbs, and the demanding nature of the routes, making it a favorite for experienced riders seeking adventure.

Q: Are there any loop trails for road cycling in Lena?
A: Yes, Lena offers several excellent loop trails. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067272422/angliru-and-cordal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Angliru and Cordal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 34.9 miles (56.2 km) long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067288804/la-cobertoria-and-gamoniteiro-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 53.6 miles (86.2 km) route.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Lena?
A: The duration for road cycling routes in Lena varies significantly based on distance and elevation. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067272422/angliru-and-cordal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Angliru and Cordal Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours and 15 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067288804/la-cobertoria-and-gamoniteiro-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take about 6 hours and 30 minutes due to its length and significant climbs.

Q: Are there any specific challenges or considerations for road cycling in Lena?
A: Road cycling in Lena is defined by its challenging mountainous terrain, featuring significant elevation gains and demanding ascents. Cyclists should be prepared for high-altitude climbs and steep descents. It's advisable to check weather conditions, especially in mountain passes, as they can change rapidly.

Q: Is Lena, Illinois, a good place for road cycling?
A: The Lena in question for these road cycling routes is characterized by mountainous terrain, which aligns with the region of Lena in Asturias, Spain, known for its challenging climbs. If you are referring to Lena, Illinois, the Lake Le-Aqua-Na State Recreation Area offers multi-use trails suitable for various cycling types, including road cycling on paved or well-maintained paths within the park or surrounding areas, but it does not feature the same mountainous challenges as the Lena described in this guide.

Q: Can I find routes that include specific mountain passes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Lena incorporate famous mountain passes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tuiza de Arriba – La Cubilla Pass loop from Tuíza&lt;/a&gt; directly features &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering a challenging and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that offer views of waterfalls?
A: Yes, some routes in the Lena area may pass by natural monuments, including waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/102603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xiblu Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the region that could be incorporated into a scenic ride.
tips: Good shortcut from Angliru to Gamoniteiro perfectly rideable with zero traffic. You may encounter cows and goats on the road so watch your speed. Has a few steep climbs but the shortcut and views are still worth the effort.

Normally done in descent from Puerto de La Cobertoria to the town of Santa Marina along a quiet road in good condition that offers us great views of the surroundings. We can make a stop at the Forcichave Viewpoint that we will find during the descent.

As we climb higher, we see the cloud filling the valleys below us, evoking an otherworldly environment that, combined with our fatigue, creates something magical. In many ways, it's a more visually impressive climb than Angliru.

faqs.question: Is the La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered very difficult and is best suited for experienced cyclists. Both the La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro climbs feature steep and sustained gradients, with sections reaching up to 17%.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions like on this route?
faqs.answer: The road surface on the La Cobertoria section is generally excellent and wide. However, after turning towards Gamoniteiro, while largely very good, you will encounter short sections (around 100-200m) of concrete that can be quite rough, almost like riding over cobbles. These often coincide with the steepest parts of the climb. Approximately 85% of the loop is asphalt.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect on the La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a typical Asturian landscape with green, lush mountains. As you ascend, the scenery becomes more isolated and spectacular, offering opportunities to see clouds filling the valleys below. From the summit of Alto de Gamoniteiro, you're rewarded with stunning 360-degree panoramic views, including the Ubiñas Natural Park, Redes, and the central area of Asturias. Expect to see pastures, small sheepfolds, rocky areas, and livestock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring to early autumn when the weather is more stable and the mountain passes are clear of snow. During these months, you can enjoy the beautiful yellow flowers and avoid the challenges of winter cycling in the Asturian mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from Pola de Lena, where you can find parking options. This town serves as a convenient base for accessing the climb.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de Las Ubiñas-La Mesa. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private land.

faqs.question: Is the La Cobertoria and Gamoniteiro Climb Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the surrounding natural park may allow dogs on leash, this specific route is a challenging road cycling loop. It is not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists on the ride itself due to the nature of the sport and the demanding terrain. If you plan to visit the area with a dog, consider other activities or trails more appropriate for pets.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or highlights will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the challenging ascent to Alto de Cuchu Puercu – Espines Pass, the scenic Road from Puerto de La Cobertoria to Santa Marina, and the ultimate reward of reaching the summit of Alto de Gamoniteiro, which offers unparalleled 360-degree views and is one of the highest roads in Spanish Asturias.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with or runs alongside several other paths. Notably, it includes sections of the official trails such as [CIMA AS11] La Cobertoria - Gamoniteiro * Santa Marina / Llanuces, [CIMA AS11] La Cobertoria - Gamoniteiro * Pola de Lena, and Ruta de San Melchor.

faqs.question: How does the difficulty of Gamoniteiro compare to Angliru?
faqs.answer: The Gamoniteiro is often compared to the nearby Angliru in terms of its demanding nature and steep gradients. While both are legendary Asturian climbs featured in the Vuelta a España, Gamoniteiro is described as a 'monstrous ascent' with sections up to 17%, particularly challenging in its final kilometer. It offers a similar level of extreme difficulty, though it may not reach Angliru's absolute most extreme gradients.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or accommodation near the starting point?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pola de Lena, the typical starting point for this loop, offers various facilities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. It's a good place to refuel before or after your challenging ride.

waypoints.name: Alto de Cuchu Puercu – Espines Pass
Road from Puerto de La Cobertoria to Santa Marina
